current_strategy_code:
import pandas as pd
from crabquant.indicator_cache import cached_indicator

DEFAULT_PARAMS = {
    "rsi_long_len": 50,
    "rsi_short_len": 7,
    "regime_threshold": 50,
    "dip_level": 35,
    "exit_level": 55,
    "ema_len": 50,
}

DESCRIPTION = "RSI regime filter with dip timing. Long RSI determines bullish regime. Short RSI times entry on dips below dip_level in bullish regime. Exits when short RSI recovers above exit_level or regime turns bearish. Mean-reversion within trends."


def generate_signals(df: pd.DataFrame, params: dict | None = None) -> tuple[pd.Series, pd.Series]:
    p = {**DEFAULT_PARAMS, **(params or {})}
    close = df["close"]

    rsi_long = cached_indicator("rsi", close, length=p["rsi_long_len"])
    rsi_short = cached_indicator("rsi", close, length=p["rsi_short_len"])
    ema = cached_indicator("ema", close, length=p["ema_len"])

    bullish_regime = (rsi_long > p["regime_threshold"]) & (close > ema)
    dip_signal = rsi_short < p["dip_level"]

    entries = (bullish_regime & dip_signal).fillna(False)

    recovery = rsi_short > p["exit_level"]
    regime_break = (rsi_long < p["regime_threshold"]) | (close < ema)
    exits = (recovery | regime_break).fillna(False)

    return entries, exits

previous_attempts.hypothesis: AAPL exhibits strong momentum trends that can be captured by combining MACD direction with RSI timing to avoid overbought entries, confirmed by volume. ATR trailing stops will protect against drawdowns while letting winners run.

The original strategy generated only 1 trade because requiring MACD histogram to cross zero exactly is extremely restrictive. Relaxing entry to MACD histogram simply being positive (above zero) rather than crossing zero will dramatically increase trade frequency while still capturing momentum. Adding an EMA trend filter ensures we only enter in established uptrends, and using RSI for exit timing provides better risk management than ATR trailing stops which can whipsaw.

MACD-based momentum has failed twice on AAPL. The MACD histogram is too lagging and noisy for this ticker. Switching to ROC (Rate of Change) for direct momentum measurement, which is faster and less smoothed than MACD. Combined with EMA trend filter and volume confirmation for signal quality. ATR trailing stops will let winners run instead of exiting on every MACD histogram dip, which was causing premature exits and killing profit factor. ROC+EMA+Volume has demonstrated Sharpe 2.21 in historical backtests.
